page: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation with Clang. Memory performance is also notable, with read-only small block memory bandwidth placing in the top tier, while large block read and write bandwidths rank in the top 25 percent. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves top-tier performance in small-model LLM prompt processing and strong performance in medium-model prompt processing and text generation, though performance drops to average levels for large 70B models.

From a resource density perspective, the r7a.12xlarge offers high memory capacity paired with dedicated compute cores, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. However, the high-speed DDR5 memory and strong CPU instruction support, including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, offset this by delivering high throughput for in-memory processing.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications such as large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, Redis deployments, and software compilation. It is also capable of handling moderate LLM inference tasks and data processing workloads that benefit from high memory-to-CPU ratios and dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and small-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong results in database operations and medium-model LLM inference. The absence of local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage. This instance is suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 19 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 19 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 RAM for demanding in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical AMD EPYC cores running at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and software compilation
No local storage or GPU hardware included
